name: B2B Payment Reconciliation SaaS
target_customer: Finance manager at SMB or mid-market company processing 50-500 vendor invoices per month
problem_statement: Finance teams spend hours weekly matching bank statements against invoices because payment confirmations arrive without invoice numbers and remittance data travels separately from payments.
trigger_signal: BCG, QED, and Coinflow all independently cite reconciliation as the top unsolved B2B payment pain in 2025-2026 reports
evidence_summary: Industry consensus from BCG Global Fintech Report, QED 2026 predictions, Coinflow blog, and Bringg 2025 last-mile analysis
path_to_first_revenue: Build SMB reconciliation API, sell to 3 pilot finance teams in 60 days at $500/mo

name: SMB Last-Mile Logistics Integration Layer
target_customer: Operations manager at SMB e-commerce brand with 10-200 daily shipments needing real-time sync between logistics and ERP
problem_statement: Every logistics platform generates data other systems depend on, yet none auto-connects logistics execution to the SMB ERP or OMS. Integration is the weakest link regardless of which logistics tool is used.
trigger_signal: SuiteFleet 2026 identifies ERP-to-logistics integration gap as the single most common complaint across all logistics software categories
evidence_summary: SuiteFleet 2026 logistics software report, Last-mile delivery market research from ResearchAndMarkets and BusinessResearchInsights
path_to_first_revenue: Integration connector for Shopify + Onfleet as first paid pilot, 90 days

name: ESG Compliance SaaS for Mid-Market
target_customer: Sustainability manager at mid-market manufacturing or retail company facing mandatory ESG disclosure deadlines in EU or US
problem_statement: ESG reporting is mandatory in 50+ countries but enterprise-grade tools cost $50K+/year. Mid-market firms ($10M-$500M revenue) are doing carbon tracking and supplier compliance in spreadsheets.
trigger_signal: ESG reporting mandates expanding globally as of 2025. Enterprise tools priced out of mid-market. Regulatory deadline-driven demand with clear ROI.
evidence_summary: Pedals Up microniche SaaS analysis 2025, Qubit Capital vertical SaaS report 2026
path_to_first_revenue: 3 pilot customers from sustainability consultant networks, $2K/mo, 60 days

name: Skilled Trades Vertical Recruiting Platform
target_customer: Construction contractor or facilities management company hiring 5-50 skilled tradespeople per month
problem_statement: Contractors cannot find qualified electricians, plumbers, and HVAC technicians via generic job boards that ignore trade certifications, licensing, and union membership.
trigger_signal: Qubit Capital 2026 identifies skilled trades as top underserved vertical. Millions of skilled workers, high churn, employers paying high CAC to wrong platforms.
evidence_summary: Qubit Capital vertical SaaS 2026, Pedals Up microniche SaaS, medium.com microniche analysis
path_to_first_revenue: Free trial for 3 contractors, monetize at $200/mo per hiring seat after first placement

name: AI Relationship Manager for Community Banks
target_customer: Head of SMB banking at community bank with $500M-$5B in assets trying to retain SMB book against fintech competition
problem_statement: Community banks competing against nationals need AI-augmented RM tools for SMB clients with real-time cash flow insights, but no affordable tool exists below enterprise tier.
trigger_signal: QED 2026 predictions: B2B banking infrastructure for community banks is next wave. AI-augmented RM cited as key gap by multiple VCs.
evidence_summary: QED Investors 2026 fintech and VC predictions, BDO 2026 fintech industry predictions
path_to_first_revenue: Pilot with 2 community banks via banker conference introductions, $5K/mo, 90 days
kill_reason: Kill gate triggered: 3 of 7 criteria failed (threshold: 2). Failed: KG-03, KG-05, KG-06.

name: Embedded Credit for Informal LATAM SMBs via SaaS
target_customer: Informal LATAM SMB owner with $500-$10K monthly revenue rejected by banks but with 12+ months digital transaction history
problem_statement: 87% of micro and small businesses in LATAM have unmet credit demand. Vertical SaaS platforms sit on behavioral data that could unlock embedded credit but don't use it.
trigger_signal: $1.8T credit gap in LATAM. R2 Capital proven model: embed credit into SaaS using transaction data. 87% of MSMBs credit-unmet per OECD 2024.
evidence_summary: R2 Capital embedded credit research, QED top 10 LatAm fintech trends 2025, OECD 2024 SMB Policy Index, Hustle Fund LATAM analysis
path_to_first_revenue: White-label credit product for 1 LATAM vertical SaaS platform, 90 days, revenue share model

name: Vertical SaaS OS for Informal LATAM Retail SMBs
target_customer: Owner of informal retail shop in Colombia, Mexico, or Peru doing $1K-$20K monthly, managing inventory via WhatsApp and notebooks
problem_statement: Nearly half of LATAM businesses operate informally, owners spending 20+ hours per week on manual invoicing and cash flow. No tool targets the informal-to-formal transition for physical retail.
trigger_signal: OECD 2024: 50% of LAC businesses informal, 88% microenterprises. 20+ hrs/week wasted on manual ops. Status quo identified as biggest competitor.
evidence_summary: OECD SMB Policy Index 2024, PaymentsCMI SaaS in LATAM analysis, R2 Capital SMB research, Hotmart $1B benchmark
path_to_first_revenue: WhatsApp-native onboarding of 10 informal retailers in Bogota, $15/mo, within 30 days

name: Spanish-Language Creator Monetization Stack
target_customer: Spanish-speaking content creator with 10K-500K followers in Mexico, Colombia, or Argentina earning under $1K/month despite large audience
problem_statement: LATAM creators lead global digital consumption but capture only 5.2% of global creator economy revenue. No platform combines localized payment rails and direct-to-fan tools for Spanish-speaking creators.
trigger_signal: LATAM creator economy growing at 27% CAGR to 2031. 58% of creators cite monetization challenges. No integrated Spanish-language monetization stack exists.
evidence_summary: Future.com LATAM creator economy deep dive, CommuniPass creator monetization 2026 report, Coherent Market Insights, Epidemic Sound report
path_to_first_revenue: Launch subscription tier for 100 Spanish creators via DM campaign, $10/mo, 45 days

name: LATAM B2B Stablecoin Settlement with Accounting Integration
target_customer: LATAM SMB owner doing $50K-$2M annual revenue with cross-border suppliers, already using Binance P2P manually
problem_statement: LATAM SMBs are settling B2B invoices in USDT to avoid currency volatility, but no compliant tool combines stablecoin settlement with local accounting and tax reporting.
trigger_signal: LATAM SMBs using stablecoins for B2B settlement confirmed in multiple 2025 sources. Venezuela uses USDT for 80% of oil exports. Zero compliant accounting tool exists.
evidence_summary: Coinflow B2B fintech trends 2026, Criptonoticias USDT Venezuela analysis, BDO 2026 fintech predictions
path_to_first_revenue: 3 pilot LATAM SMBs via crypto Telegram groups, $50/mo compliance tool, 60 days

name: USDT Accounting Tool for Venezuelan Informal SMBs
target_customer: Venezuelan small business owner doing $5K-$50K annual revenue in USDT, afraid of SENIAT formalization but aware of compliance risk
problem_statement: Venezuela processed $51B in informal USDT payments in 2025 yet 3.5M+ entrepreneurs operate with zero fiscal traceability. No tool helps VE businesses track USDT flows for SENIAT compliance without punitive tax exposure.
trigger_signal: Criptonoticias 2025: $51B informal USDT payments. SENIAT prioritizing crypto enforcement. Government announces banking USDT integration Dec 2025. Zero affordable compliance tool.
evidence_summary: Criptonoticias economia invisible USDT 2025, BeinCrypto Venezuela USDT escasez 2025, observatorio blockchain USDT Venezuela analysis
path_to_first_revenue: WhatsApp + Telegram outreach to VE business groups, $8/mo USDT tracking tool, first 20 users in 30 days

name: WhatsApp-Native Order Management for Venezuelan Retail SMBs
target_customer: Venezuelan small retailer (food, clothing, electronics) doing $1K-$15K monthly in USDT via WhatsApp, managing 20-200 orders per day manually
problem_statement: 55% of Venezuelan commerce is informal and WhatsApp-first. Retailers manage catalogs, orders, and USDT payment confirmation entirely via WhatsApp DMs, losing 10+ hours per week to manual ops.
trigger_signal: VE informal commerce 55% off-platform. WhatsApp penetration near 100% among smartphone users. Retailers sending product photos in groups, zero catalog or order management tools.
evidence_summary: Observatorio Blockchain USDT Venezuela deep dive, Criptonoticias VE merchant USDT usage, beincrypto VE USDT 2025 analysis
path_to_first_revenue: DM outreach to 5 WhatsApp commerce group admins, offer free setup, monetize at $15/mo after 2 weeks

name: Venezuelan P2P Rate Optimization Tool for Merchants
target_customer: Venezuelan merchant or service provider processing $5K-$100K monthly in USDT who loses 2-5% daily to suboptimal P2P routing decisions
problem_statement: Venezuelan merchants manually navigate a 75%+ gap between official BCV rate and market rate daily. No tool monitors best P2P rates across Binance, Crixto, and Kontigo or auto-routes for optimal rate and fee.
trigger_signal: 75%+ official/parallel rate gap persists. Merchants doing manual Binance P2P daily. Zero rate optimization tool exists despite 80% of VE oil exports paid in USDT.
evidence_summary: Criptonoticias VE merchants USDT analysis, Yahoo Finance USDT Venezuela correction, observatorio blockchain USDT Venezuela
path_to_first_revenue: Telegram bot MVP: fetch best rate across 3 P2P platforms, $10/mo or 0.2% routing fee, 2 weeks to first user

name: Venezuelan Diaspora Remittance-to-Investment Platform
target_customer: Venezuelan diaspora member in US, Spain, or Colombia sending $200-$2K/month to family and wanting to invest in VE assets without wire transfer friction
problem_statement: 6M+ Venezuelans abroad send remittances home but have no tool to convert inbound USDT flows into productive VE assets. Money arrives and is spent immediately with no wealth-building mechanism.
trigger_signal: VE diaspora 6M+. Arranca model proves diaspora willing to invest back in GPS-collateralized assets. Zero platform connecting diaspora USDT to local productive assets.
evidence_summary: BeinCrypto Venezuela USDT analysis 2025, Criptonoticias USDT as oxygen for VE economy 2025
path_to_first_revenue: First diaspora investors via VE WhatsApp communities, USDT escrow for vehicle financing, $50 fee per investment, 60 days

name: Diaspora-to-Venezuela Payroll and Contractor Management Tool
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: Venezuelan businesses hire diaspora talent; diaspora businesses hire Venezuelan remote workers. Deel ($12B) doesn't serve Venezuelan contractors well (no compliant entity in VE). Gap: USDT payroll + compliance wrapper for VE-Spain, VE-Colombia, VE-US corridors at $20-50/contractor/month vs Deel $49+.
trigger_signal: First 10 customers: Spanish companies using Venezuelan remote developers (Source: LinkedIn Venezuela remote work trends 2024)

name: USDT Merchant Payment Rails and Accounting for Venezuelan Commerce
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: Venezuelan commerce is USDT-native. Merchants accept USDT daily but have NO tooling: no POS, no accounting integration, no customer receipts, no reconciliation. #1 globally in USDT adoption per capita (Chainalysis 2024). Binance P2P Venezuela $3B+ annual volume. WTP: $5-15/month.
trigger_signal: Current workflow: send screenshot of transaction as receipt (Source: Chainalysis Geography of Crypto 2024)

name: E-Commerce Trust and Escrow Layer for Venezuelan Digital Commerce
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: Venezuelan online commerce broken by fraud. Both buyer and seller afraid. No PayPal, Stripe, or consumer protection. Gap: lightweight escrow layer (hold USDT, release on confirmation) for C2C and C2B digital transactions. Enables e-commerce economy that currently can't exist. Estimated $500M Venezuelan digital commerce TAM.
trigger_signal: Exact Reddit complaint: 'nadie compra en Instagram porque no hay proteccion' (Source: Reddit r/vzla 2024)

name: Venezuelan Remittance Corridor Digitization
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: Venezuelan diaspora (7M people, 75+ countries) sends $6B/year to family in Venezuela. 80% via informal channels. Fee savings: 6-8% Western Union vs 0.5-1% USDT rails = $300-450M/year capturable. Key corridors: Peru→VE, Chile→VE, Colombia→VE, US→VE, Spain→VE.
trigger_signal: AirTM and Reserve already serve this but not WhatsApp-native (Source: BCVF Remittance Data 2024)

name: SMB Inventory and POS for Venezuelan Informal Retailers
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: Venezuelan retailers (abastos, farmacias, electronics tiendas) run inventory on notebooks or WhatsApp. Saint ERP exists but expensive ($200+/month, overkill). Gap: simple inventory + POS + supplier ordering at $5-15/month. Bodega tech pattern proven globally (Kabbage, Kirana Club in India). 10K retailers in Caracas alone.

name: Bodega Tech Stack for Venezuelan Neighborhood Stores — Plan Bodega Signal
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: Plan Bodega government program reached 5,000+ bodegas in Venezuela. Private tech stack for bodegas: inventory, ordering, USDT + Zelle + bolivar payment acceptance, basic accounting. 300K+ bodegas in Venezuela. Average daily revenue $50-200. WTP: $8-12/month.
trigger_signal: Government distributed inventory tablets but no software — private opportunity is more reliable (Source: Plan Bodega Venezuela MPCYDC 2024)

name: Venezuelan Freelancer Cross-Border Payment Stack
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: 400K+ Venezuelan freelancers on Upwork, Fiverr, remote jobs. Getting paid is the #1 problem: no PayPal, no Stripe, no bank wire. Current workaround: Wise → family abroad → cash out. Need: USDT collection + invoice generation + tax compliance wrapper. Scored 7.75 in previous run.
trigger_signal: Pain is extreme and daily (Source: Reddit r/vzla digital workers)

name: Venezuelan Necessity Entrepreneurship Infrastructure — Meta-Signal
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: GEM 2024-2025: 93.6% of Venezuelan entrepreneurs started due to job loss — highest necessity entrepreneurship rate globally. 1.8M active businesses. These entrepreneurs need: business tools, credit, digital payment acceptance, supplier access, customer acquisition. Any tool solving one = massive TAM with extreme urgency.

name: BNPL and Consumer Credit Access for Venezuelan Market — Cashea Signal
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: Cashea: Venezuela BNPL, 7.5M users, 15%/week growth, proven product-market fit. Adjacent: white-label BNPL infrastructure for Venezuelan retailers. Alt credit scoring without formal credit bureau data = biggest technical moat — use payment history, WhatsApp activity, referral network as proxy.

name: Informal Vendor Digitization — Street Vendor POS and Digital Presence
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: LATAM has 50M+ informal vendors (ambulantes, tianguis, ferias) with zero digital presence, cash-only, no inventory tracking. QR-based POS + digital catalog + basic CRM at $0-10/month. Clip (Mexico) $100M raised. Above-payment layer = open. Colombia: 48% employment is informal.
trigger_signal: Key behaviour: vendors already use Instagram + WhatsApp for sales — need tools extending these (Source: ILO Informal Economy Report 2024)

name: Venezuelan Informal Commerce and Social Commerce OS
target_customer: Venezuelan SMB owner or informal entrepreneur using USDT/Zelle
problem_statement: 55% of Venezuelan commerce is informal. Instagram DMs and WhatsApp groups = primary sales channel for 500K+ micro-businesses. No catalog, no order tracking, no payment confirmation, no delivery coordination. P2P Rate Aggregator signal also in this cluster. Scored 7.75 in previous run.
trigger_signal: 6% of Venezuelan entrepreneurs started due to job loss (GEM 2024) (Source: GEM Venezuela 2024-2025)

name: E-Invoicing Compliance SaaS for Mandatory LATAM Regulations
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: Colombia (DIAN), Mexico (SAT), Chile (SII), Peru (SUNAT) all have mandatory electronic invoicing. Non-compliance = tax penalties. SMBs scrambling to comply. Alegra, Siigo serving this but fragmented. $2-10/month per company x 30M SMEs = massive TAM.

name: WhatsApp Commerce OS for Latin American SMBs
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: Latin American SMBs already sell on WhatsApp informally. No software layer for catalog management, order tracking, inventory, payment collection, follow-up. $18.2B market. Mercately raised $2.6M seed. Treble, Sirena, Kommo competing but fragmented. WhatsApp penetration 91-97% across LATAM.

name: Last-Mile Distribution Management for Independent LATAM Sales Reps
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: LATAM $450B FMCG distribution relies on 2M+ independent reps who carry physical catalogs, collect cash, manually input orders. Colombia: 350K reps. Mexico: 500K+ tiendita supply chains. Mobile-first order management + cash collection + route planning = unaddressed gap.
trigger_signal: B2B2Rep model: FMCG brands pay per active rep (Source: Nielsen LATAM FMCG Distribution Study)

name: SMB Cybersecurity — Managed Security for Non-Technical Businesses
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: SMBs represent 43% of cyberattack targets but have no dedicated tooling. Crowdstrike too expensive ($50K+). Gap: managed security at $200-500/month per business. 60% of SMBs close within 6 months of breach. Average breach cost $108K for <250 employees.
trigger_signal: Distribution via insurance brokers is most capital-efficient channel (Source: Verizon DBIR 2024)

name: Creator Monetization for Micro-Creators (Sub-$15K Earners)
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: 95% of creators earn <$15K/year. Patreon/Substack built for established creators. Gap: monetization infrastructure for micro-creators (1K-50K followers). Stan.store $8M ARR. LATAM Spanish-language gap: TikTok + Instagram creator density high, monetization tools English-only.
trigger_signal: $250B creator economy by 2027 (Source: Linktree Creator Economy Report 2024)

name: Stablecoin Remittance Rail for Central America and Caribbean
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: $174.4B total LATAM remittances/year. USDC/USDT rails cut fees from 6-8% (Western Union) to 0.1-0.5%. Bitso $2.2B valuation. Corridor: US→MX, US→Guatemala, Venezuela→Colombia growing. Western Union still charges 6-8% = $10.5B/year in fees available to capture.
trigger_signal: Bitso powered several LATAM corridors (Source: World Bank Remittance Prices 2024)

name: B2B BNPL for SME Supply Chain in LATAM
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: SME suppliers to large retailers wait 60-120 days for payment while paying their own suppliers in 15-30 days. Working capital gap = 3-4 months of revenue. B2B BNPL at 2-4%/month. Kontempo raised $31.5M, Fairplay $35M, Addi $200M. Colombia and Mexico most advanced.
trigger_signal: Defensibility: proprietary credit model from transaction data (Source: Kontempo pitch deck 2024)

name: SMB Talent Management SaaS — HR for 10-200 Employee Companies
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: SMBs with 10-200 employees can't afford Workday or SAP but Excel is broken. Rippling, Gusto, Factorial serve this gap. LATAM penetration <5%. Payroll + time tracking + compliance bundled = sticky product at $8-20/employee/month.
trigger_signal: Factorial (Spain/LATAM) reached $80M ARR (Source: G2 HRIS Category Q1 2026)

name: SME Expense Management for LATAM — Loto LatAm Signal
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: LATAM SMEs manage expenses with paper receipts and Excel. Loto LatAm raised $1M pre-seed for Mexico SME expense management. Ramp/Divvy analog but LATAM-native. 30M registered SMEs in LATAM with zero expense management tooling.
trigger_signal: Key gaps: peso/USDT/USD multi-currency management, CFDI (MX) and DIAN (CO) invoice compliance, WhatsApp receipt capture (photo → categorized expense) (Source: Loto LatAm pre-seed announcement)

target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: 40M smallholder farmers in LATAM lack credit access. Formal banks require 3 years audited accounts. Alt-data scoring (satellite imagery + weather + yield history + market prices) enables agricultural credit at 2-3x lower risk. IDB estimates $85B agri-credit gap in LATAM.
trigger_signal: Agrotoken (Argentina) $15M Series A using grain as collateral (Source: CGAP Agri-Finance 2024)

name: Cross-Border SMB Parcel Shipping — Consolidated Freight for Small Merchants
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)

No freight broker serves sub-$50K/month shippers with dynamic rate comparison + customs automation + tracking. LATAM: Colombia-US, Venezuela-Colombia freight = high-margin informal market needing formalization.
trigger_signal: Cross-border ecommerce $6 (Source: Flexport Index Q4 2025)

name: Micro-Insurance for LATAM Gig Workers
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: LATAM gig workforce: 45M+ workers. Insurance penetration <8% vs 12% global. One accident = financial devastation. Microinsurance at $2-10/month via employer platform (Rappi, Uber, Didi). Regulatory risk: insurance requires license. Venezuela: mototaxistas, mandaderos = 3M+ addressable with USDT premium collection.

name: MSME Credit Gap — Underserved SME Lending Infrastructure
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: 170M MSMEs globally lack access to formal credit. Digital lenders using alternative data achieve 2-4% NPL vs 8-12% bank average. $5.2T gap. Winners use embedded distribution (accounting software, payment platforms) not direct acquisition.

name: Supply Chain Data Integration — Multi-System Unification for Mid-Market
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: Companies running multiple ERPs can't get unified supply chain view. Manual ETL costs $500K+/year in consultant hours. Integration layer at $30-100K/year replaces pain. SMB version: WhatsApp + Sheets + QuickBooks = chaos. Pre-built connectors for LATAM ERPs (Aspel, Monica, SIIGO) = moat.
trigger_signal: 95% of supply chain issues traced to data visibility failures (Source: IDC Supply Chain Tech Report 2025)

target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: LATAM fintechs face cascading AML/KYC requirements. Manual compliance = $2-5K per compliance officer/month + high error rate. Colombia SARLAFT (AML) mandatory for all financial entities since 2022. Venezuela on FATF grey list = enhanced DD mandatory. No LATAM-native RegTech leader.
trigger_signal: Unit21, Hummingbird in US (Source: FATF LATAM Assessment 2024)

target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: SaaS companies growing past $1M ARR need ASC 606/IFRS 15 revenue recognition automation. QuickBooks breaks. NetSuite is $50K+. Gap: $500-2000/month sub-ledger that syncs Stripe/Chargebee to accounting with automatic deferred revenue. Recognized.com, Ordway emerging.

target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: Colombia launched mandatory open finance regulations 2024 (SFC mandate). Banks must expose APIs by 2025. No aggregation layer built yet. PSD2 in Europe created 500+ fintechs on top of banking APIs. Colombia = 12-18 months behind. $50M+ first-mover opportunity.

problem_statement: PLG SaaS companies lose 85-95% of free users before first payment. No tooling orchestrates upgrade moments intelligently (usage triggers + intent signals + right CTA). Median PLG free-to-paid = 4-7%. Top quartile = 12-15%. Gap is the upgrade moment layer.

target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: 60% of hourly workers live paycheck to paycheck. EWA platforms let workers access earned wages before payday for $1-5/transaction. Employer-sponsored = zero cost to employee. $12B market by 2027. LATAM expansion underway.

problem_statement: Next SaaS wave: single vertical, AI-native from day 1, replacing 2-5 legacy point solutions. Harvey (legal), Suki (healthcare), Arini (dental AI). Pattern: start with one repetitive document workflow, expand to full ops layer. Spanish-language AI now at parity with English.

name: AI Logistics OS for Mid-Market Distributors

problem_statement: Mid-market distributors (100-1000 employees) can't afford SAP but need AI forecasting. Current solution: Excel + phone calls. name: AI Fraud Detection for LATAM Payment Rails
target_customer: Latin American SMB owner or fintech founder in Colombia, Mexico, or Argentina
problem_statement: LATAM payment fraud 3x higher than global average. PSPs and neobanks need fraud ML that understands local patterns (CPF/CURP identity fraud, PSE manipulation, Pix fraud in Brazil). name: SMB On-Demand Logistics — Flexible Last-Mile for Small Merchants
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: SMBs can't commit to fixed logistics contracts but need reliable same-day delivery. Platforms like Lalamove, Rappi for Business bridge the gap. LATAM: informal motorcycle fleets dominate but lack software layer. $47B last-mile market growing 18% YoY.
trigger_signal: Stuart profitable in EU (Source: McKinsey Last-Mile 2024)

name: Fintech Gender Gap — Women-First Financial Products in Emerging Markets
target_customer: Founder or operator of a growing SMB (10-200 employees)
problem_statement: Women control 80% of household purchasing decisions but receive <10% of traditional financial products. Niche: savings, insurance, credit for women in emerging markets. 2.4B underbanked women globally. LATAM gap = 15pp below men.

name: B2B Cross-Border Payment Rails Gap
target_customer: global Fintech operators and founders
problem_statement: While stablecoins dominate B2C payments, B2B banking faces significant barriers from regulatory fragmentation and disparate global systems. No scalable solution yet bridges these gaps for SMB cross-border transactions.
trigger_signal: B2B banking faces significant barriers with regulatory fragmentation and disparate global systems creating friction that stablecoins alone cannot solve. QED Investors identifies this as a major market gap for 2026.

name: Alternative Credit Scoring for Gig Workers
target_customer: global Fintech operators and founders
problem_statement: Traditional credit scores leave 49M Americans without loan access. Gig workers and freelancers lack solutions showing real-time earning capacity and cash flow stability.
trigger_signal: Traditional credit scores reflect historical data not real-time earnings, leaving 49 million Americans without loan access. Alternative credit scoring for gig workers is an underserved market.

name: Financial Forecasting Automation for SMBs
target_customer: global Smb Software operators and founders
problem_statement: 80% of SMBs still use manual spreadsheets for financial planning at $28,500 annual cost per employee in manual entry. Cash flow forecasting market growing 7.4% CAGR but adoption remains low.
trigger_signal: Manual data entry costs US companies $28,500 per employee annually. Global cash flow forecasting software market projected $726M in 2025, 7.4% CAGR through 2033. Most SMBs still on spreadsheets.

name: Credential Management for Non-Tech SMBs
target_customer: global Smb Software operators and founders
problem_statement: 52% of SMBs still manage passwords and credentials in spreadsheets. No purpose-built credential management exists for small businesses without enterprise overhead.
trigger_signal: 52% of SMBs rely on manual tools like spreadsheets to manage sensitive credentials. Most SMBs find enterprise PAM tools too expensive and complex. Clear gap between free tools and enterprise solutions.

name: Vertical SaaS for Construction Site Operations
target_customer: global B2B Saas operators and founders
problem_statement: Construction firms still rely on clipboards and spreadsheets for site management and scheduling. Vertical SaaS for construction remains underserved despite growing 250%+ faster than horizontal SaaS.
trigger_signal: Construction firms transitioning from spreadsheets and clipboards to software. Vertical SaaS in construction growing 250%+ faster than horizontal SaaS. Thousands of industries still running on Excel and paper.

name: Last-Mile Route Optimization for Regional Couriers
target_customer: global Logistics operators and founders
problem_statement: Regional SMB logistics operators face 8% failed first-attempt delivery rates costing $17.20 per order. Manual routing without real-time optimization for 20-30 stops creates massive inefficiency.
trigger_signal: 8% of first-attempt home deliveries fail costing $17.20 per order on average. SMBs struggle with route optimization for 20-30 stops per shift without real-time systems. Failed delivery reattempts consume labor and damage customer satisfaction.

name: Creator Merch and Fulfillment Integration Platform
target_customer: global Creator Monetization operators and founders
problem_statement: Creators wanting to sell merchandise must manually integrate separate tools for design, inventory, fulfillment, and shipping. No unified solution handles end-to-end merch operations with creator economics.
trigger_signal: Creators must go to multiple platforms for merch — no unified solution handles design, inventory, fulfillment, and customer management. Gaps between creator platforms getting harder to ignore for merch and community features.

name: LATAM SMB Embedded Credit and Financing Layer
target_customer: LATAM Fintech operators and founders
problem_statement: 9 in 10 SMBs underserved across LATAM with $1 trillion financing gap. Opportunity to embed credit directly into SMB distribution channels — ERPs, marketplaces, B2B platforms.
trigger_signal: SMBs account for 90%+ of LATAM businesses but face risk-averse incumbent banking with $1T financing gap. Embedded finance projected $39B in 2025, growing $60B+ by 2030. SMB fintech adoption growing 25%+ CAGR through 2025.

name: Colombian Vertical SaaS for Financial Services Ops
target_customer: LATAM B2B Saas operators and founders
problem_statement: Colombia represents 12% of LATAM SaaS market with 20%+ annual growth. Strong fintech talent drives demand for vertical SaaS in financial operations, accounting, and payments.
trigger_signal: Colombia posts 20%+ growth on smaller base vs mature Brazil/Mexico. 98% of LATAM enterprises are SMEs seeking affordable digital transformation. IT/Telecom vertical leads with 25% market share in 2025.

name: Mexican Marketplace Embedded Finance Stack
target_customer: LATAM Fintech operators and founders
problem_statement: Mexico is 20% of LATAM SaaS market with surge in 2025 investment. B2B marketplaces seeking embedded credit, accounts, insurance, and payments directly into workflow.
trigger_signal: Mexico emerging as second-largest LATAM fintech hub with VC surge 2025. Trend focuses on infrastructure for embedded accounts, credit, insurance in marketplaces and ERPs. Open banking and AI driving innovation.

name: LATAM Creator Monetization via Stablecoins
target_customer: LATAM Creator Monetization operators and founders
problem_statement: Content creators across LATAM face banking and payment friction. Opportunity to build creator payment networks using USDT/USDC for instant borderless monetization with local currency withdrawal.
trigger_signal: Venezuelan e-commerce increasingly digital-first. Digital payment adoption high at 96% fintech penetration but fragmented. Stablecoins enable sovereign digital commerce without corporate platform intermediaries.

trigger_signal: 47% of Venezuelan sub-$10k transactions use stablecoins. Pago Movil CRIXTO enables direct USDT payments to any merchant. Venezuela mobilized $44.6B in digital assets in 2025. Merchants view USDT as solution to currency shortage and lack of formal banking access.

name: Venezuelan BNPL Expansion to LATAM Markets
target_customer: Venezuelan Fintech operators and founders
problem_statement: Cashea (Venezuela's first BNPL) proves the model works for LATAM underbanked segments. Expansion to Colombia, Argentina, and Mexico where BNPL adoption is nascent among informal merchants.
trigger_signal: Cashea is Venezuela's first BNPL with active user base. Pagoplace secured 3,000+ merchant partnerships across Caracas, Maracaibo, Valencia as of 2025. Venezuelan fintech raising capital and scaling within constraints — proving informal market viability.
